review_expected_but_resolved.summary: Mostly healthy auto-resolutions where the classifier attached an obvious title directly; treat as baseline under-labeling noise, not a blocker.

expected_id_missing.summary: Rows already carrying authoritative IDs in the baseline are still dropping into lookup because canonical/series matching misses them.

content_id_mismatch.summary: A small but high-signal set: some are real matcher bugs, some are guardrail misses, and a large share are polluted baseline IDs.

type_mismatch.summary: Manual review says 2/3 are baseline type pollution and 1/3 is a real classifier drift.

no_baseline_needs_lookup.summary: Most rows simply lack baseline labels, but sampled anomalies show that guardrail leakage hides in this bucket.

no_baseline_rejected.summary: Sampled rejects looked justified; keep this as a non-regression safety bucket rather than a fix target.

rootCauseGroups.summary: Canonical lookup is missing obvious known titles when site tags, punctuation, localized text, or extra subtitle text stay in the title candidate.
suggestedFollowups: Tighten shared_series_parser and movie-title candidate cleanup before index lookup.
Expand series/movie alt-title coverage for localized/transliterated names before punting to needsTmdbLookup.
Add prefix/alias fallback only after stronger normalization, so obvious canonical families resolve without broad false positives.

rootCauseGroups.summary: Generic movie titles are being resolved by weak title/year/popularity signals, producing wrong exact IDs instead of deferring or finding the correct family.
suggestedFollowups: Use stronger disambiguation for same-title same-year collisions (IMDb/original-title evidence when available).
Penalize weak popularity-only wins for generic one-word/two-word titles.
Prefer lookup over a low-confidence exact ID when the normalized title is highly ambiguous.

manualLowerBounds.note: These are conservative keyword heuristics over the 10,450-row run, not gold-label counts.

rootCauseGroups.summary: Obvious courseware, adult previews, and sports/event dumps are surviving as movie rows instead of being rejected or fenced off before canonical matching.
suggestedFollowups: Broaden courseware/topic detection beyond English-only brand lists.
Add sports/event pack rejection or separate non-canonical sink before movie matching.
Tighten adult-preview leakage checks on teaser/preview-style payloads that still carry a single MP4.

rootCauseGroups.summary: Anime episode/batch structures are sometimes slipping into the movie path, which then either misses or attaches the wrong franchise.
suggestedFollowups: Strengthen anime episode/batch detection before movie fallback.
Keep anime collections in TV/anime_pack handling even when extras or CD folders are present.

rootCauseGroups.summary: Some exact-ID baseline rows are plainly wrong on title evidence, so they should be excluded from regression expectations until the source data is cleaned.
Two of the three type mismatches are baseline mistakes where anime packs were labeled as movies; they are useful audit examples but not product regressions.
This bucket mostly shows that the baseline still expects manual review even when the current classifier can resolve obvious titles cleanly.
